pi_items.source: setup_task.sh creates file at this location (line 81)
setup_task.sh creates export directory (line 9), export_result.sh checks for PDF at this location
setup_task.sh creates file at this location (line 13)
Direct analysis of debate_structure.txt created by setup_task.sh. Central claim is 'Immediate 6-Month Pause on Giant AI Experiments' which contains 'pause' and '6-month' but NOT 'ban' or 'six-month'

Direct analysis of supporting arguments in debate_structure.txt: 'Risk of Flooding Information Channels with Propaganda' (propaganda, flood), 'Loss of Human Control over Civilization' (control), 'Lack of Regulatory Frameworks' (regulatory, framework)

Direct analysis of opposing arguments in debate_structure.txt: 'Geopolitical Disadvantage vs Adversaries' (geopolitical, adversar), 'Stifles Scientific Innovation & Medical Breakthroughs' (stifle, innovat), 'Implementation is Technically Infeasible' (technically, infeasible)

Direct analysis of rebuttal text in debate_structure.txt: 'International Treaties Can Mitigate Geopolitical Risks' (treaties, mitigate), 'Existing AI Safety Guidelines are Sufficient' (guidelines, sufficient)
GitHub API check shows draw.io v26.0.9 exists and was released on 2025-01-27. Confirmed in install_drawio.sh line 52-54
debate_structure.txt specifies 'SUPPORTING Links: Color = Green (#00CC00), Style = Solid Line'
debate_structure.txt specifies 'OPPOSING Links: Color = Red (#FF0000), Style = Dashed Line'

privileged_info_summary: The task involves creating a synthetic AI regulation argument map in draw.io with 8 specific nodes (1 central claim, 3 supporting arguments, 3 opposing arguments, 2 rebuttals) and specific styling (green solid arrows for support, red dashed arrows for opposition). The debate structure is entirely generated by the setup script. Draw.io version 26.0.9 (released January 27, 2025) is used. Color codes are #00CC00 for supporting arguments and #FF0000 for opposing arguments. Supporting argument keywords are 'propaganda', 'flood', 'control', 'regulatory', 'framework'. Opposing argument keywords are 'geopolitical', 'adversar', 'stifle', 'innovat', 'infeasible', 'technically'. Rebuttal keywords are 'treaties', 'mitigate', 'guidelines', 'sufficient'. However, the central_keywords metadata incorrectly includes 'ban' and 'six-month' which do not appear in the actual central claim text ('Immediate 6-Month Pause on Giant AI Experiments') - only 'pause' and '6-month' appear.
